skill.name: qwen-image-2-pro
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI. The skill focuses on text-heavy visuals, posters, banners, and controlled image prompts.
skill.summary: Create professional text-heavy images with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns because the skill documents use of the infsh CLI. These are legitimate for the stated image-generation purpose, but they send prompts, reference image URLs, and job data to inference.sh. Weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections were false positives from prose, parameter names, and documentation tables; no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Generation CLI Sends User Content Externally
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run infsh commands and Python SDK calls against alibaba/qwen-image-2-pro. This is expected behavior, but prompts, reference image URLs, and generated image requests leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and SDK examples directly invoke a remote inference app. The usage is legitimate for this skill, but it creates a clear network data exposure risk.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly Expected CLI Examples
low_findings.description: The repeated shell detections come from documented infsh and npx commands. The command surface is constrained to infsh in the tool declaration, and I did not find arbitrary shell construction or hidden execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible CLI documentation rather than executable code embedded in the package. The allowed tool pattern limits execution to the infsh command family.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk static labels do not correspond to cryptographic APIs, hashing functions, or system reconnaissance logic. The cited lines contain model descriptions, prompt parameters, output tables, and prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation and no host reconnaissance command. The static scanner appears to match words inside documentation tables and descriptions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Access to External CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands. This is necessary for the workflow, but users should know that running it can start remote inference jobs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit. The risk depends on user prompts and local infsh configuration, so the finding is moderate rather than high.

content.user_title: Create Qwen Pro Images with Text
content.value_statement: Text-heavy image generation often fails when titles, labels, or layouts must be exact. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro image generation through the infsh CLI.
Creates prompts for posters, banners, logos, and product visuals.
Supports image editing with one to three reference image URLs.
Documents size, seed, watermark, negative prompt, and prompt extension options.
Shows Python SDK usage for single requests and streamed progress updates.
limitations: Requires an installed and authenticated inference.sh CLI or SDK.
Sends prompt content and image references to the inference.sh service.
Generated text can still need review and regeneration for exact typography.
The skill does not include its own image model or local renderer.

use_cases.title: Design Marketing Posters
use_cases.description: Create prompts for sale banners, event posters, and social graphics with specified text placement.

use_cases.title: Prototype Brand Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate multiple visual directions for logos, product mockups, and campaign assets.

use_cases.title: Edit Images with References
use_cases.description: Use reference image URLs to describe style transfer or outfit and object changes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2-pro to create a 1024 by 1024 image of [subject] in [style]. Keep the composition clean.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Text Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Poster with controlled title and subtitle
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a poster with the exact title "[title]" and subtitle "[subtitle]". Specify font style, color, placement, background, and negative prompt for distorted text.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several directions from one concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [number] variations for [campaign or product]. Use the same prompt and seed strategy so I can compare layout, color, and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Edit with Reference Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these reference image URLs: [urls]. Edit the main subject so it adopts [change], while preserving identity, lighting, and realistic texture.

output_examples.input: Create a summer sale banner with exact discount text.
output_examples.output: A ready-to-run infsh command with a 1920 by 1080 prompt, negative prompt, and prompt extension disabled.

output_examples.input: Generate four logo concepts for a coffee shop.
output_examples.output: A Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro request that sets the brand name, style direction, and four image variations.

output_examples.input: Edit a person image using an outfit reference URL.
output_examples.output: A reference-image editing request that preserves the person while applying the outfit from the second image.

best_practices: Put exact visible text in quotes and describe where it should appear.
Disable prompt extension when the wording must remain precise.
Use negative prompts for blurry text, distorted letters, and low quality.
anti_patterns: Do not include private or regulated information in image prompts.
Do not expect perfect text in the first generation for complex layouts.
Do not use reference image URLs that you are not allowed to process.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates or edits images with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it run locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI or SDK to run a remote image model.

faq.question: What is it best at?
faq.answer: It is best for posters, banners, and image prompts that need clearer rendered text.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented input supports one to three reference image URLs for editing tasks.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private prompts?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and reference URLs may be sent to the inference.sh service.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
